sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current frame, I can see the black F and charcoal F are now very close - they're almost touching. The black F is now right next to the charcoal F. Let me check the segmentation to see their exact positions and whether they're overlapping.
From the images:
- The charcoal F is on the left side
- The black F is right next to it, slightly to the right
- The yellow F is on the far right
It seems like the black F has moved closer to the charcoal F. They might be overlapping now. Let me check if pressing SPACE does something when they overlap, or if I need to move them further.
Let me inspect the current state to see if the black and charcoal F are overlapping.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
        maxr = max(p[0] for p in b)
        minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
        maxc = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} id={n['id']} bbox=[{minr},{minc}]-[{maxr},{maxc}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good, now I can see the objects clearly:
- Charcoal F (c): id=5, bbox=[45,24]-[53,29]
- Black F (B): id=6, bbox=[45,30]-[53,38]
- Yellow F (Y): id=7, bbox=[45,51]-[53,59]
The charcoal F and black F are now adjacent (charcoal ends at col 29, black starts at col 30). They're touching but not overlapping. Let me try pressing SPACE to see if that triggers something, or I need to move them to overlap.
Actually wait - looking at the images, the black F and charcoal F are side by side. The black F's top bar seems to be touching the charcoal F's top bar. They're adjacent horizontally.
Let me try pressing SPACE to see if this adjacency triggers the level completion.

action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
